title: BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations and breast cancer risk: updated meta-analysis
abstract: Germline pathogenic variants in BRCA1 and BRCA2 confer substantially elevated lifetime risks of breast and ovarian cancer. This updated meta-analysis synthesizes data from 55 studies and 271,000 carriers. BACKGROUND: BRCA1 carriers have a 72% lifetime breast cancer risk; BRCA2 carriers have 69%. Risk modification by polygenic background is now well-established. METHODS: We performed PROSPERO-registered meta-analysis of cohort studies. RESULTS: Risk-reducing surgery (bilateral mastectomy) reduces breast cancer risk by 90%. PARP inhibitors show efficacy in BRCA-mutated metastatic breast cancer. CONCLUSIONS: Cascade testing of relatives of BRCA carriers should be standard of care.
